panels.description: Car's own estimate of time remaining until the charge target is reached.
panels.title: Time to target

panels.description: The ChargePoint is the source of truth — the car only refreshes its own charging-power reading every few minutes.
panels.title: Charging power

panels.description: The ChargePoint resets this counter at the start of each session, so every sawtooth tooth is one charging session.
panels.title: Energy per session

panels.description: Rolling 24-hour totals. increase() is reset-aware, so it sums correctly across the ChargePoint's per-session counters.
panels.title: Energy & cost, rolling 24 h

panels.description: How fast range is being replenished. The ChargePoint's mi/h is an estimate and tends to run optimistic versus real-world consumption.
panels.title: Charge rate

panels.description: Total energy pulled from the wall over the selected time range.
panels.title: Energy delivered

panels.description: Charging cost over the selected time range, as reported by ChargePoint.
panels.title: Charging cost

panels.description: Odometer delta over the selected time range.
panels.title: Miles driven

panels.description: Miles driven ÷ energy taken from the wall. This is wall-to-wheel, so it includes charging losses and is lower than the car's own trip-computer efficiency. Only meaningful over ranges long enough to contain both driving and charging.
panels.title: Efficiency (wall-to-wheel)

panels.description: Charging cost ÷ miles driven over the selected range.
panels.title: Cost per mile

panels.description: Total time the ChargePoint spent actively charging over the selected range.
panels.title: Time spent charging

panels.description: Range added by the charger versus miles actually driven, both as rolling 24-hour totals. A persistent gap means the charger's mi/kWh assumption is optimistic for how you actually drive.
panels.title: Miles driven vs range added, rolling 24 h

panels.description: Rolling 7-day wall-to-wheel efficiency. Smoother than the range-scoped stat above and useful for spotting seasonal drop-off.
panels.title: Efficiency, rolling 7 d

panels.description: Doors, windows, hood and trunk. 1 means open — the aggregate Doors and Windows sensors roll up the individual ones.
panels.title: Openings

panels.description: Door, trunk and charge-plug latches. These invert relative to the openings above: 1 means UNLOCKED. The plug latch reads locked while a session is live, which is why it sits at 0 during charging.
panels.title: Locks

panels.description: Plain on/off states — 1 means active. Unlike the locks these are informational rather than a warning: a connected plug or a moving car is not a fault condition.
panels.title: Plug, motion & lights

panels.description: Miles remaining until the next scheduled service inspection.
panels.title: Service in (distance)

panels.description: Days remaining until the next scheduled service inspection.
panels.title: Service in (time)

panels.description: How stale the car's telemetry is. The Audi connect API only refreshes periodically, so a few tens of minutes is normal; hours means the car has gone quiet.
panels.title: Telemetry age

panels.description: How long the car has been parked, derived from its reported park time.
panels.title: Parked for
